relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH speaks holy-food boundaries.
Priests must handle Israel's holy gifts without profaning the Name.
Approaching holy food while unclean ruptures the boundary.
Certain conditions pause eating holy food until cleansing.
Contact states are named as ritual uncleanness.
Washing and evening mark return.
After sunset he may eat, for it is his food.
Carcass and torn animal are avoided.
Priests keep the charge of the holy.
Holy food belongs within priestly household boundaries.
Ancient household members may eat priestly food.
A priest's daughter joined to another household no longer eats it.
If she returns without seed, she may eat her father's food again.
Unintentional eating is repaired with added fifth.
Israel's holy gifts are not to be profaned.
YHWH sanctifies the holy-food order.
YHWH speaks offering acceptability.
Native and sojourner bring vow and freewill offerings.
The offering is complete for acceptance.
A blemished offering is not brought for acceptance.
Peace/well-being offerings for vow or freewill are complete.
Animal blemish categories are restricted from altar offering.
Some irregular animals may be freewill offerings, not vows.
Mutilated animals are not brought near.
These offering-boundaries are not bypassed through foreign hands.
YHWH speaks care for newly born animals.
The newborn stays seven days under its mother.
Mother and young are not slaughtered in one day.
Thanksgiving is sacrificed for acceptance.
The thanksgiving meal is eaten that day.
Keep and do the commandments.
YHWH is sanctified in Israel's midst.
The One who sanctifies is the One who brought them out of Egypt.

contemplative_note: Leviticus 22 keeps holy food and offerings within ritual integrity, yet ends where Torah keeps returning: the Sanctifier is the Liberator from Egypt.
